statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simply the complex fraction \frac{{\frac{1}{m-1}+\frac{3}{m-1}}}{{3-\frac{1}{m+1}}}, we multiple the numerator and denominator with the common denominator of {\frac{1}{m-1}+\frac{3}{m-1}} and {3-\frac{1}{m+1}}, which is {\left(m+1\right)\mathopen{}\left(m-1\right)}.
Since m+1 was canceled, m \ne -1 is a lost domain condition.
Although m-1 was also canceled, m \ne 1 is not a lost domain condition because it is implied in the solution.
